2605.09907.title: RADAR: Redundancy-Aware Diffusion for Multi-Agent Communication Structure Generation
2605.09907.paper_summary: Compared with individual agents, large language model based multi-agent systems have shown great capabilities consistently across diverse tasks, including code generation, mathematical reasoning, and planning, etc. Despite their impressive performance, the effectiveness and robustness of these systems heavily rely on their communication topology, which is often fixed or generated in a single step. This restricts fine-grained structural exploration and flexible composition, resulting in excessive token utilization on simple tasks while limiting capability on complicated tasks. To mitigate this challenge, we introduce RADAR, a redundancy-aware and query-adaptive generative framework that actively reduce communication overhead. Motivated by recent progress in conditional discrete graph diffusion models, we formulate communication topology design as a step-by-step generation process, guided by the effective size of the graph. Comprehensive experiments on six benchmarks demonstrate that RADAR consistently outperforms recent baselines, achieving higher accuracy, lower token consumption, and greater robustness across diverse scenarios. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/cszhangzhen/RADAR.
2605.09907.paper_summary_zh: 與單一代理相比，基於大型語言模型的多代理系統在代碼生成、數學推理和規劃等多樣任務中展現了卓越的能力。儘管它們的表現令人印象深刻，但這些系統的有效性和穩健性在很大程度上依賴於其通信拓撲，而這通常是固定的或在單一步驟中生成的。這限制了細緻的結構探索和靈活的組合，導致在簡單任務上過度使用標記，而在複雜任務上則能力受限。為了緩解這一挑戰，我們引入了RADAR，一種具冗餘感知和查詢自適應的生成框架，能主動減少通信開銷。受到近期條件離散圖擴散模型進展的啟發，我們將通信拓撲設計公式化為逐步生成過程，並以圖的有效大小為指導。在六個基準上的綜合實驗表明，RADAR在各種場景中始終超越近期基準，實現了更高的準確性、更低的標記消耗和更大的穩健性。我們的代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/cszhangzhen/RADAR 獲得。
2605.09907.author: Zhen Zhang et.al.
2605.09907.authors: Zhen Zhang, Wanjing Zhou, Juncheng Li, Hao Fei, Jun Wen, Wei Ji
